| Regional cup apps  = 14

| Regional cup best  = Runners-up ([[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]])

| 2ndRegional cup best = Runners-up ([[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]], [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]])
|socks3=000000|shorts3=000000|rightarm3=000000|body3=000000|leftarm3=000000|pattern_ra3=_mli23t|pattern_b3=_mli23t|pattern_la3=_mli23t}}

The '''Mali national football team''' ([[Bambara language|Bambara]]: '' Mali jamana ntolatantɔn'', {{langx|fr|Équipe du Mali de football}}) represents [[Mali]] in men's international [[association football|football]] and is governed by the [[Malian Football Federation]]. The team's nickname is ''Les Aigles''. They represent the country at tournaments organized by both [[FIFA]] and the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).

While Mali is a major youth football power in both Africa and the world, they have never qualified for any senior [[FIFA World Cup]] finals in history. They have qualified for the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] on 14 occasions.

Mali were suspended by FIFA on 17 March 2017 due to 'government interference' with the national football association, namely dissolving its executive committee.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/governance/news/y=2017/m=3/news=fifa-suspends-malian-football-association-femafoot-2876348.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170318172609/http://www.fifa.com/governance/news/y=2017/m=3/news=fifa-suspends-malian-football-association-femafoot-2876348.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=18 March 2017|title=FIFA Suspends Malian Football Association (FEMAFOOT)|publisher=FIFA|access-date=7 April 2017|date=17 March 2017}}</ref> However, the side was re-instated by FIFA on 29 April after the executive committee was re-introduced by the Malian government.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/39758233|title=Caf [''sic''] confirm Mali's suspension has been lifted by Fifa [''sic'']|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=23 November 2018|archive-date=24 November 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181124055330/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/39758233|url-status=live}}</ref>

==History==
Mali reached the [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972 African Nations Cup]] final, but lost 3–2 to [[Congo national football team|Congo]]. They failed to qualify for the finals again until [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]] when they reached the semi-finals, an achievement repeated in [[2002 African Cup of Nations|2002]], [[2004 African Cup of Nations|2004]], [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]] and 2013.

They played their first [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] [[FIFA World Cup qualification|qualifier]] in the year [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round#Pool C|2000]]. As part of the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|CAF's qualifiers]] for the [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002 World Cup]], Mali lost in the preliminary round to [[Libya national football team|Libya]]. Two years later, the country hosted the 2002 African Cup of Nations.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-shot.com/africa/african-nations-cup-2002/qualification.html|title=Mali 2002 - The African Cup Of Nations XXIII - The Qualification|website=the-shot.com|publisher=The Shot|access-date=2 July 2014|archive-date=11 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211111212648/http://www.the-shot.com/africa/african-nations-cup-2002/qualification.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

Mali's under-23's team managed to qualify for the [[2004 Summer Olympics]] in [[Greece]]. The team coached by [[Cheick Kone]] managed to reach the quarter-finals of [[Football at the 2004 Summer Olympics&nbsp;– Men's tournament|the Olympic tournament]] before losing to [[Italy national under-21 football team|Italy]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/tournaments/archive/mensolympic/athens2004/news/newsid=93862/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714115900/http://www.fifa.com/tournaments/archive/mensolympic/athens2004/news/newsid=93862/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=14 July 2014|title=Italy down brave Mali in extra time (1:0)|website=FIFA.com|date=21 August 2004|publisher=Fédération Internationale de Football Association|access-date=2 July 2014}}</ref>

In the [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006 World Cup]] [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|qualifiers]], Mali beat [[Guinea-Bissau national football team|Guinea-Bissau]] in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|preliminaries]]. In the resulting [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group 1|second round]], Mali finished fifth in its group. On 27 March 2005, riots broke out in [[Bamako]] after Mali lost a World Cup qualifier to [[Togo national football team|Togo]], 2–1 on a last minute goal.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/sports/soccer/world/2005-03-28-world-cup-roundup_x.htm|title=Mali soccer fans burn cars, riot after World Cup qualifier|website=usatoday.com|publisher=The Associated Press|date=28 March 2005|access-date=2 July 2014|archive-date=11 November 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211111212320/http://usatoday30.usatoday.com/sports/soccer/world/2005-03-28-world-cup-roundup_x.htm|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010 African Nations Cup]], Mali made football headlines for coming back from losing 4–0 with eleven minutes left to level 4–4 with [[Angola national football team|Angola]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8450491.stm|title=Angola 4–4 Mali|work=BBC Sport|date=10 January 2010|access-date=3 February 2013|archive-date=10 January 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220110130422/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8450491.stm|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|AFCON of 2025]] Mali defeated Tunisia on penalties, reaching the quarter finals against Senegal, without losing a single match in the whole tournament.<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.theguardian.com/football/live/2026/jan/03/mali-v-tunisia-africa-cup-of-nations-last-16-live|title=NThe 10 men of Mali stunned Tunisia in the Casablanca rain with a dramatic victory via penalty kicks|publisher=[[The Guardian]]|language=en|date=3 January 2026}}</ref>

===Team kit===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
! Manufacturer
! Period
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Kappa (company)|Kappa]] ||1998–2003
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Baliston (Company)|Baliston]]||2003–2007
|-
|align="Left"|{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Airness]] ||2007–
|}

== Rivalries ==

=== Tunisia ===
[[File:Confrontation Mali - Tunisie.jpg|thumb|Mali v Tunisia at the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations]].]]
The rivalry between Mali and [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] has been evident in recent years in terms of the number of frequent matches, especially in the [[Africa Cup of Nations]]. The two teams met for the first time on 6 January 1974, during a friendly match in [[Bamako]], which ended with a 1–0 victory for Mali.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Mali vs Tunisia - 1974-01-06 - FIFA Friendlies - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1974-01-06/mali-vs-tunisia/1860660/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> The match between the two teams in the [[1994 African Cup of Nations]], which was held in Tunisia, remains one of the biggest setbacks witnessed by the Tunisian national team, as Mali won the match 2–0,<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Tunisia vs Mali - 1994-03-26 - Africa Cup of Nations - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/1994-03-26/tunisia-vs-mali/328101/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> which led to Tunisia's early exclusion from the tournament that was held on its soil. In recent years, the team's matches have continued, as they met in the last three consecutive editions of the African Cup of Nations [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]], [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]] and [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]], all of which were in the group stage,<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Tunisia vs Mali - 2019-06-28 - Total Africa Cup of Nations - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2019-06-28/tunisia-vs-mali/1626546/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Tunisia vs Mali - 2022-01-12 - TotalEnergies Africa Cup of Nations - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2022-01-12/tunisia-vs-mali/2452056/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> where Mali won two matches and one ended in a draw.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Tunisia vs Mali - 2024-01-20 - TotalEnergies Africa Cup of Nations - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2024-01-20/tunisia-vs-mali/3196945/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> The two teams met in the third and [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF third round|final round]] of the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2022 World Cup qualification]]. Two round-robin matches were played. However, Tunisia qualified for the World Cup after winning the first leg in Bamako 1–0 with an own goal from defender [[Moussa Sissako]],<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Mali vs Tunisia - 2022-03-25 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2022-03-25/mali-vs-tunisia/2628055/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> and a goalless draw prevailed in the return match in [[Radès]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Match Report of Tunisia vs Mali - 2022-03-29 - WC Qualification - Global Sports Archive |url=https://globalsportsarchive.com/match/soccer/2022-03-29/tunisia-vs-mali/2628058/ |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=globalsportsarchive.com}}</ref> This was Mali's closest chance of qualifying for the World Cup.<ref>{{Cite web |last=AP |date=2021-11-12 |title=World Cup Qualifiers: Mali through to last 10 in WC qualifying, setback for Ghana |url=https://sportstar.thehindu.com/football/mali-through-to-last-10-in-world-cup-qualifying-setback-for-ghana-fifa-world-cup-qualifiers/article37450859.ece |access-date=2024-03-22 |website=Sportstar |language=en}}</ref> The competition record remains with Tunisia with a slight advantage. Tunisia won 6 matches, 3 of which ended in draws, and Mali won 5 matches.

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Position !! Name
|-
| Head coach
| {{Flagicon|Portugal}} [[Anthony da Silva]]
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{Flagicon|Mali}} Alou Badra Diallo
|-
| Goalkeeper coach
| {{Flagicon|Mali}} Bounafou Doumbia
|-
| Fitness coaches
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} Riedoh Berdien<br>{{flagicon|France}} Alexandre Coppolani<br>{{flagicon|Mali}} Bougadary Sangare
|-
| Team doctor
| {{flagicon|Mali}} Dr. Halidou Maiga 
|-
| Video analyst
| {{flagicon|Belgium}} Gregory Verheyden<br>{{flagicon|Ireland}} Stephen Oonan
|-
| International Scouts
| {{flagicon|Italy}} Alessandro Soli<br>{{flagicon|Germany}} Robby Echelmeyer

|-
|}

===Coaching history===
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Ben Oumar Sy]] (1960–66)
*{{flagicon|HUN}} [[György Tóth]] (1966–70)
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Karl-Heinz Weigang]] (1970–73)
*{{flagicon|USSR}} [[Mykola Holovko]] (1979–82)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Kidian Diallo]] (1982–89)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Molobaly Sissoko]] (1989–93)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Mamadou Keïta]] (1993–97)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Christian Sarramagna]] (1998–2000)
*{{flagicon|ITA}} [[Romano Mattè]] (2000–01)
*{{flagicon|POL}} [[Henryk Kasperczak]] (2001–02)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Christian Dalger]] (2002–03)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Henri Stambouli]] (2003–04)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Alain Moizan]] (2004)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Mamadou Keïta]] (2004–05)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Pierre Lechantre]] (2005–06)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} ''[[Amadou Pathé Diallo]]'' (2006)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Jean-François Jodar]] (2006–08)
*{{flagicon|NGA}} [[Stephen Keshi]] (2008–10)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Alain Giresse]] (2010–12)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} ''[[Amadou Pathé Diallo]]'' (2012)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Patrice Carteron]] (2012–13)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} ''[[Amadou Pathé Diallo]]'' (2013)
*{{flagicon|POL}} [[Henryk Kasperczak]] (2013–15)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Alain Giresse]] (2015–17)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Mohamed Magassouba]] (2017–2022)
*{{flagicon|MLI}} [[Éric Chelle]] (2022–2024)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[Tom Saintfiet]] (2024–2026)
*{{Flagicon|POR}} [[Anthony da Silva]] (2026–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] match against [[Iran national football team|Iran]] on 4 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/FemafootOfficial/posts/pfbid02kgFUiP9BeBpBa8B7Py29Wrnb2CAKjffn2RCYp7iVKAwPWtYf5CpXeHpmvg9kCrCSl|title=Le sélectionneur des Aigles par intérim, Badra Alou Diallo a dévoilé ce samedi, la liste des joueurs convoqués pour affronter l’Iran 🇮🇷 en match amical le jeudi 4 juin 2026 à Antalya en Turquie|publisher=[[Malian Football Federation]]|date=23 May 2026|access-date=2 June 2026|language=French|via=Facebook}}</ref>
<br>''Caps and goals correct as of 31 March 2026, after the match against [[Russia national football team|Russia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs break}}
<sup>DCL</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br/>
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br/>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br/>
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|22 December 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Mali - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/mali-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=9 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221209225121/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/mali-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Mali.''

|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!0
!0
!0
!0
!0
!0
!58
!24
!16
!18
!78
!59
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Mali at the Africa Cup of Nations}}

Mali have never won the Africa Cup of Nations; their best result at the tournament was runners-up in [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]. Mali have qualified for the tournament fourteen times in total, finishing as runners-up once, third twice, and fourth three times. Prior to 2008, each time Mali qualified, they reached the knockout stage having only qualified four times before then.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
! colspan="10" |[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! rowspan="41" width="1" |
! colspan="6" |Qualification record
|-

!|{{Abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!|{{Abbr|GA|Goals against}}

===African Nations Championship===
Mali has competed in five [[African Nations Championship]] tournaments, finishing as runners-up twice.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
! colspan="9" |[[African Nations Championship]] record
! width="1" rowspan="9" |
! colspan="6" |Qualification record
|-

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[Africa Cup of Nations]]'''
** [[File:Silver medal africa.svg|15px]] Runners-up (1): [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
** [[File:Bronze medal africa.svg|15px]] Third place (2): [[2012 African Cup of Nations|2012]], [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
* '''[[African Nations Championship]]'''
** [[File:Silver medal africa.svg|15px]] Runners-up (2): [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]], [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]
* '''[[Football at the African Games|African Games]]'''<sup>'''1'''</sup>
** [[File:Silver medal africa.svg|15px]] Silver medal (1): 1965

===Regional===
* '''[[Amilcar Cabral Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (3)''': 1989, 1997, 2007
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (4): 1979, 1981, 1987, 1988
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (5): 1982, 1983, 1984, 1985, 2001
* '''[[UEMOA Tournament]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (3): 2008, 2011, 2016

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[African Cup of Nations|CAF African Cup of Nations]]
|0 || 1 || 2 || 3
|-
|align=left|[[African Nations Championship|CAF African Nations Championship]]
|0 || 2 || 0 || 2
|-
